title: Hohenbergia ridleyi
taxonomy: Hohenbergia ridleyi is classified as a species within the genus Hohenbergia, which belongs to the family Bromeliaceae. The Bromeliaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pineapple family. This classification places Hohenbergia ridleyi in the order Poales, which includes various monocotyledonous plants. The family Bromeliaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, and Hohenbergia ridleyi's classification within these subfamilies is still being researched. Further study is necessary to fully understand its taxonomic relationships within the Bromeliaceae family.

intro: Hohenbergia ridleyi is a species of bromeliad, a type of epiphytic flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Hohenbergia ridleyi include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hohenbergia ridleyi is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
